subject: Sociology
title: Culture and Values
description: Learn Culture and Values in one minute.

hook.text: In Japan, slurping noodles is polite. In America, it's rude. Same act, different meaning. That's culture at work.

buildup.text: Culture is the shared beliefs, values, symbols, and practices that define a group. It shapes everything you do.

discovery.text: Values are abstract ideals (freedom, equality). Norms are the concrete rules that translate values into behavior.

twist.text: Cultures often hold contradictory values. Americans prize equality and meritocracy, yet inequality persists.

climax.text: Culture feels natural to insiders and strange to outsiders. That gap reveals how deeply it shapes perception.

punchline.text: Culture is the water you swim in — invisible until you leave it.

quiz.question: What is the relationship between values and norms?
options: Values are abstract ideals; norms are concrete rules based on them
They are identical concepts with different names
